title: Refrigerated medicine outage decision record
summary: Preserve time and temperature information and obtain product-specific advice instead of guessing whether a refrigerated medicine remains usable.
content:
Preserve time and temperature information and obtain product-specific advice instead of guessing whether a refrigerated medicine remains usable.

Action sequence: Keep the refrigerator closed and record the outage start time. Copy the medicine name, strength, form, storage range, batch, expiry, and whether it froze or was exposed to heat. Contact the pharmacist, prescriber, manufacturer, or emergency service for product-specific instructions. Separate questionable stock, label its exposure history, and replace it as soon as advised. Safety boundary: Do not discard a life-sustaining medicine or change its use without product-specific professional advice when help is reachable. Do not place medicine directly on ice or freeze it unless its official instructions explicitly allow that.
steps: Keep the refrigerator closed and record the outage start time.
Copy the medicine name, strength, form, storage range, batch, expiry, and whether it froze or was exposed to heat.
Contact the pharmacist, prescriber, manufacturer, or emergency service for product-specific instructions.
Separate questionable stock, label its exposure history, and replace it as soon as advised.
warnings: Do not discard a life-sustaining medicine or change its use without product-specific professional advice when help is reachable.
Do not place medicine directly on ice or freeze it unless its official instructions explicitly allow that.

title: Seven-day medicine continuity sheet
summary: Create a portable, private record that lets a pharmacy or clinician quickly understand regular medicines and critical supply gaps.
content:
Create a portable, private record that lets a pharmacy or clinician quickly understand regular medicines and critical supply gaps.

Action sequence: List generic and brand name, strength, form, usual schedule, reason, prescriber, pharmacy, allergies, and last dose. Mark medicines that must not be stopped suddenly and those requiring refrigeration, monitoring, or special devices. Count the usable supply and write the date each item will run out at the usual schedule. Keep one protected paper copy with the person and one copy with a trusted contact. Safety boundary: Do not share the full list publicly or with people who do not need it. Do not ration, split, substitute, or change doses without qualified advice.

title: Poisoning call preparation in Greece
summary: Collect the container, exposure details, symptoms, and person information while immediately contacting the Greek Poison Centre or 112 as appropriate.
content:
Collect the container, exposure details, symptoms, and person information while immediately contacting the Greek Poison Centre or 112 as appropriate.

Action sequence: For collapse, breathing trouble, seizure, severe burns, or immediate danger call 112. Otherwise call the Greek Poison Centre at 210 7793777 and follow its instructions. State age, approximate weight, substance, amount, route, time, symptoms, first actions already taken, and exact location. Keep the product and packaging available and note every instruction and time. Safety boundary: Do not induce vomiting or give food, drink, medicine, or a home remedy unless the Poison Centre or emergency professional instructs it. Do not enter a contaminated area or touch the substance without appropriate protection.

title: Emergency pharmacy handover note
summary: Give a pharmacist a concise verified record so urgent continuity questions can be solved without relying on memory.
content:
Give a pharmacist a concise verified record so urgent continuity questions can be solved without relying on memory.

Action sequence: Separate confirmed information from what the person only remembers approximately. Present current medicines, allergies, last doses, remaining supply, storage exposure, and the exact reason continuity is at risk. Ask the pharmacist to write product-specific instructions, substitutions authorized by the prescriber, and the next review point. Update the household medicine sheet immediately after the handover. Safety boundary: Do not ask an unqualified person to identify loose tablets by appearance. Do not combine old and replacement instructions without confirming which plan is current.

title: Medicine go-bag rotation without missed doses
summary: Rotate emergency medicine stock through normal use with a written handover so the bag stays current without creating double dosing or shortages.
content:
Rotate emergency medicine stock through normal use with a written handover so the bag stays current without creating double dosing or shortages.

Action sequence: Choose a fixed monthly date and one responsible person for checking expiry, damage, temperature exposure, quantity, devices, and documents. Move the oldest suitable stock into normal use only after the active supply and dosing schedule are confirmed. Replace the emergency quantity before removing the old stock and record package identifiers and new expiry dates. Have a second person compare the final bag with the current medicine list. Safety boundary: Do not store medicines in a hot vehicle, direct sun, damp bathroom, or unlabelled container. Do not create an emergency supply by skipping prescribed doses.
